flashcards.front: What architecture revolutionized LLM development in 2017?
flashcards.back: Transformer architecture with self-attention mechanisms enabled parallel processing and long-range dependencies.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between BERT and GPT architectures?
flashcards.back: BERT uses bidirectional context (masked language modeling) while GPT uses unidirectional left-to-right autoregressive modeling.

flashcards.front: What does the 'self-supervised learning' in LLMs refer to?
flashcards.back: Training using automatically generated labels from text corpus (e.g., predicting masked words or next sentence).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of positional encoding in transformers?
flashcards.back: Injects sequence position information since transformers lack recurrent/convolutional structure for word order.

flashcards.front: Name three key components of transformer architecture.
flashcards.back: 1. Self-attention layers 2. Feed-forward networks 3. Layer normalization & residual connections

flashcards.front: What is the 'attention head' in transformer models?
flashcards.back: Parallel computation units that learn different linguistic patterns through scaled dot-product attention.
To convert text into subword units (tokens) that balance vocabulary size and sequence length efficiency.
Designing input prompts to guide LLM outputs without changing model weights (zero/few-shot learning).

flashcards.front: What ethical concern arises from LLM training data?
flashcards.back: Potential perpetuation of biases present in the training corpus through generated content.

flashcards.front: What is model parallelism in LLM training?
flashcards.back: Distributing different parts of neural network across multiple GPUs to handle massive parameter counts.

flashcards.front: What does RLHF stand for in LLM training?
flashcards.back: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback - aligns model outputs with human preferences.

flashcards.front: What is the 'temperature' parameter in LLM generation?
flashcards.back: Controls randomness: higher values increase diversity, lower values make outputs more deterministic.
